entry.title: Case Management: Case Hierarchy (Main Cases and Sub-Cases)
entry.content:
Introduction:

In modern case management systems, efficient organization of information is essential for delivering fast and accurate resolutions. Businesses today deal with increasingly complex service requests, operational tasks, and customer interactions. To manage this complexity, leading organizations rely on a hierarchical case structure consisting of main cases and sub-cases. This model provides clarity, accountability, and scalability, enabling teams to divide work intelligently without losing the connection to the bigger picture. With a clear case hierarchy, companies can accelerate issue resolution, improve customer satisfaction, and create a more predictable service operation.

Imagine a customer reports a serious issue that touches multiple departments—support needs to investigate, field service must schedule a visit, and billing has to verify an error. Handling everything under one case would quickly become messy. That’s where case hierarchy shines. A main case captures the overall problem, while sub-cases help each team manage their part of the work independently, yet still stay tied to the larger story.

Configuration:

In SAP Service Cloud v2, Case Hierarchy is offered as a separate feature that customers can enable at the case-type level. It also provides flexibility, allowing configuration changes across different case-type versions. At any given time, only one version of a case type can be active.

1. Open a draft case type version in detail view

 2. In the General tab, activate the Case Hierarchy feature within the Features Settings section. Once enabled, the Case Hierarchy functionality becomes available for that specific case type.

 3. After enabling the feature, a new tab called Relationship Settings becomes available. Navigate to this tab to proceed.

 4. Select a cascade completion mechanism for main and subcases. There are three options available for the completion mechanism. The first is None, where the main case and sub-cases can be completed independently. The second option, Don't allow main case completion until all sub-cases are completed, prevents closure of the main case until its sub-cases are finished. The third option, Complete all sub-cases when the main case is completed, automatically closes all sub-cases when the main case is completed. These options are largely self-explanatory.

5. Select the appropriate options based on your requirements, then click Save to apply the changes.

6. Click Activate to activate the case type.

Case Hierarchy in Action:

Hiring a Candidate in an Enterprise Service Management Scenario: Consider the process of hiring a new candidate—a common enterprise workflow that requires coordination across several internal departments. Although it begins as a single request, the end-to-end hiring process consists of multiple activities handled by different teams. This makes it an ideal scenario for using Case Hierarchy with a main case and multiple sub-cases.

When a hiring request is submitted, a main case is created to represent the overall process: onboarding a new employee. This main case captures the core details such as candidate information, job role, joining date, and overall status.

However, completing this process requires contributions from multiple departments: 

1. IT Department — IT Setup Sub-Case

The IT team needs to prepare all the necessary resources for the new hire. A sub-case is created to handle tasks such as Laptop allocation and configuration, Email account and system access creation, Software license provisioning and Setting up workstation or remote access. This sub-case is managed independently by IT but remains linked to the main hiring case.

2. Finance Department — Payroll & Compliance Sub-Case

Finance also plays a crucial role in onboarding. Their sub-case may include: Setting up payroll for the new employee, Assigning cost centers or budgeting codes, Compliance checks for compensation and benefits and Approvals related to financial onboarding. Again, these tasks are handled within the Finance sub-case but contribute to completing the overall hiring process.

3. HR Department — HR Onboarding Sub-Case

The HR team completes several onboarding formalities, such as: Gathering and validating documents, Generating the offer letter and appointment contract, Assigning training, onboarding programs, and buddy systems, Ensuring background checks are completed and Capturing employee master data in the HR system. All of these actions are tracked in a dedicated HR sub-case.

Let’s create a case to demonstrate this scenario.

1. Create a new case with the newly activated case type and open the case in detail view.

2. To create a sub-case, click the Create button and select Sub-Case.

 3. Once the sub-cases are created, review them in the Sub-Cases section under the Related Entities tab. This tab and section are adaptation-relevant, so they must be added to the screen through Adaptation if they are not already visible.

 4. The Sub-Cases section also allows you to link an existing case as a sub-case. Click the Add Subcases button and choose the appropriate case from the value help and click on Apply.

Here, you can enter a search term, press Enter, and then select the desired case from the list.

Alternatively, click the value help icon to open the case list modal and choose the required case.

Click Apply to link the selected case as a sub-case.

It will then be displayed instantly in the related entities sub-cases section.

Use the Full Screen button to enlarge the section for better viewing.

Hover over the case card to reveal the Remove button, then click it to delink the sub-case.

For the current case type version, the completion mechanism “Don’t allow main case completion until all sub-cases are completed” is selected. This prevents the main case from being completed until all its sub-cases are resolved. If attempted to complete the main case before completing the sub-cases, a validation message is displayed.

The system will enforce the configured completion mechanism accordingly.

The hierarchy supports only one level, meaning a parent case can have sub-cases, but a sub-case cannot have its own sub-cases.

Summary:

Case Hierarchy in modern case management systems provides a structured way to manage complex processes by linking a main case with one or more sub-cases. This approach enables organizations to break down large tasks into manageable units while maintaining visibility and control over the overall workflow.

Key points:

  • Feature Enablement: In SAP Service Cloud v2, Case Hierarchy is a separate feature that must be enabled at the case-type level and can be configured differently across case-type versions. At any given time, only one version of a case type can be active.

  • Hierarchy Structure: A main case can have multiple sub-cases, but sub-cases cannot have further child cases, supporting a single-level hierarchy.

  • Completion Mechanism: The system supports configurable completion rules, such as allowing independent closure of main and sub-cases, preventing main case completion until sub-cases are finished, or automatically completing all sub-cases when the main case is closed.

  • Operational Flow: Sub-cases can be created directly or linked from existing cases. Departments like HR, IT, Finance, Facilities, Security, and, if relevant, Travel Desk (for relocations) typically manage the sub-cases, each handling specialized tasks while contributing to the main case’s resolution.

  • Benefits: Case Hierarchy enhances collaboration across departments, ensures accountability, improves tracking, and provides a clear view of progress at both sub-case and main case levels.

In essence, Case Hierarchy simplifies complex business processes by structuring work into related cases, ensuring that each task is efficiently tracked and managed while maintaining alignment with the overall objective.

entry.title: Your Path to SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 Starts Here: Understanding the DTT
entry.content:
Your Path to SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 Starts Here: Understanding the DTT

Introduction to the Data Transfer Tool (DTT)

In this blog post, I would like to introduce you to the Data Transfer Tool (DTT) in the SAP Sales & Service Cloud.
Before we dive into the tool itself, let’s take a look at the steps and prerequisites required to enable and use the DTT successfully.

Prerequisites & Activation of the Data Transfer Tool

Step 1: Create an Incident

Open an incident in SAP for Me with the appropriate component “LOD-LE-MIG-CNS”.
Important: You need to have a V2 system that is connected to your SAP Sales & Service Cloud V1 system for the transition. Both systems must be referenced in the incident.

Step 2: Enable the Tool for Administrators

Once the Data Transfer Tool has been approved, you need to enable it for your administrators or colleagues performing the transition.
Go to the Administration Settings in your V1 system and enable the “Transition” Work Center for the relevant business role.

Step 3: End User Access

After the role assignment, end users will see the Transition Work Center and gain access to the Data Transfer Tool.

Step 4: Start Working with the DTT

Once everything is set up, you can begin preparing and executing your data migration.

Note #1 – Preparation Is Key

Before performing any migration, it is highly recommended to
review the Help Page of each relevant data object. These pages contain essential information on:

✔ Prerequisites 
Example: For Cases/Tickets, the Case Types must be manually created in the V2 system before migration, so they can be mapped correctly.

✔ Restrictions
Example: For Registered Products, the migration of KUT fields is currently not supported.

✔ Data Sequences
Example: For Leads, activities and master data (such as Business Partners) must be migrated first.

Tip: Create a Migration Activity Plan to keep track 
            of dependencies, required sequences, and task responsibilities.


User Interface Overview of the Data Transfer Tool

The DTT interface provides several columns and sections that help you understand the migration status at a glance.

Column overview (left to right):

  1. Objects – List of all data objects that can be migrated via DTT
  2. More Information – Link to the object-specific Help Page
  3. Existing Instances – Number of records in the V1 system
  4. Triggered By – User who initiated the migration
  5. Triggered On – Timestamp of execution
  6. Sending Instances – Number of records sent
  7. Error Sending Instances – Error messages for failed records
  8. Send Status – Shows status (e.g. Finished when complete)
  9. Received Instances – Number of records received in the V2 system
  10. Delta Mode – Automatically activated after a successful initial migration (shown in green)
  11. Target Tenant – URL of the V2 target system

Dynamic Object Scoping: Extension Fields and Additional Details

Below the object list, additional information may appear depending on the selected object, such as:

Extension Fields (KUT Fields)

  • Node Name – Location of the field in the data model
  • Field Name – UI name
  • Usage Rate – Indicates whether the field is actively used
  • Owner – e.g. KUT
  • Transition Ability – Whether the field can be migrated via DTT
  • Transfer – Selection to include or exclude the field during migration

Transfer History

  • Listing the last migration executions for the related Data Object

Error Details

  • Listing the details of migration errors

Document Type Mapping (e.g. for Data object “Case”)

  • This is the place were you would configure the Case type mapping bevore you are executing the migration of Tickets from SAP Sales and Service Cloud to SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2.

 

Key Actions in the Upper-Right Section of the DTT

This area contains the main execution functions:

  1. Test Data Transfer – Transfers a single data object including its Business Configuration
  2. Transfer Data – Transfers all data records of the selected object plus its Business Configuration
  3. Transfer BC – Transfers only the Business Configuration
  4. Stop Delta – Deactivates the delta mechanism
  5. Start Post-Processing – Triggers object-specific follow-up processes
  6. Documentation – Access to the latest DTT documentation


Note #2 – Advantages & Limitations of the Data Transfer Tool

Advantages

  1. Business Configuration is transferred
    Unlike migration via Data Workbench, the DTT also transfers the related configuration of the data object.
  1. Preserves Object IDs and UUIDs
    The DTT keeps the same object IDs from V1, ensuring seamless continuity for integrations and historical references.
  1. Automatic Mapping
    Relationships between master data and transactional data remain intact after migration.

Current Limitations

  1. No filtering available
    The DTT currently does not support filtering records before migration—everything is transferred.

  2. Limited monitoring capabilities
    There is no detailed, record-level monitoring, making it difficult to identify missing or failed data without additional effort.

entry.title: 2025 in Utilities: Highlights, Success Stories, and What’s Next
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

Thank You for an Incredible Year!

As 2025 comes to a close, we want to extend our heartfelt gratitude to our amazing utilities community – customers, partners, colleagues, and friends. Your collaboration and commitment have powered innovation, advanced sustainability, and shaped the future of utilities.

To celebrate these achievements, we’ve prepared a special year in review video. 

👉Watch it now

Read the edition highlights of our latest newsletter:

  • SAP Utilities Roadmap for 2026 – Explore upcoming innovations and industry trends.
  • Customer Success Stories – See how leaders like X-ELIO, CVA, and Zelestra are transforming with SAP Cloud ERP and AI.
  • Event Insights – Join us in Toulouse for the SAP Energy & Utilities Conference 2026 and the new Innovation Awards.
  • Latest Solutions & Sessions – From AI-powered asset management to new features in SAP S/4HANA and Service Cloud.

👉Read the full newsletter here 
And don’t miss out on future updates! Sign up for our bi-monthly newsletter to stay ahead with insights, success stories, and event news.
👉Subscribe now 

Thank you for being part of this journey. Here’s to an innovative and sustainable 2026!

Happy Holidays from all of us at SAP Utilities!

entry.title: Empowering Teams to Streamline Sales and Service Operations with SAP Build Joule Skills
entry.content:
🌟Introduction to SAP Build Joule Skills

In today’s fast-changing business landscape, automation, intelligence, and integration are essential for success. SAP Build Joule Skills bring these elements together to help businesses optimize workflows, streamline project management, and automate repetitive processes like project opportunity creation, quote management, and customer service handling.

By combining SAP Joule’s intelligent automation with SAP’s enterprise-grade solutions, organizations can increase productivity, ensure accuracy, and empower employees to focus on high-value work.

🚨Please refer to the video for better understanding.🚨

🤖What Are SAP Build Joule Skills?

SAP Build Joule Skills are customizable automation capabilities built within the SAP ecosystem. They allow users to create intelligent workflows that respond to business needs — whether it’s creating new sales opportunities, generating quotes, or handling service requests.

✨Key Features of Joule Skills:

  • Automation: Reduce manual data entry by automating repetitive tasks.
  • Integration: Connect seamlessly with SAP and non-SAP systems.
  • Scalability: Create and manage skills that grow with your organization.
  • User-Friendly: Empower business users with low-code/no-code design tools.

🧠 What Is SAP Build Joule?

SAP Build Joule is part of the SAP Build portfolio — a low-code platform that enables business and IT users to collaboratively design, automate, and innovate processes. Joule brings an AI-powered digital assistant into this ecosystem, helping users perform complex tasks through simple, conversational interactions.

🪄 Key Benefits:

  • Accelerated automation – Build and deploy workflows faster.
  • No-code customization – Design automation logic without needing deep technical skills.
  • AI-driven insights – Use AI to make decisions faster and improve efficiency.

🌟Story:

It was another busy morning at Conva Engineering. Adrian Chase, an account manager, had a full agenda — new project sites to manage and quotes to prepare for contractors working across the site, and a customer issue waiting for resolution.

In the past, this would have meant hours of manual coordination: filling out spreadsheets, sending multiple emails, and ensuring every quote aligned with customer requirements. But since his company adopted SAP Joule, Adrian’s workday looks very different.

He starts his day by opening SAP Joule, the AI-powered assistant integrated within the company’s SAP landscapes.

“Joule, please create a new project.”

Joule instantly responds:

“Sure, please provide the site name, customer, and materials to be quoted.”

Adrian enters the details: the site name “Free Text”, the customer details, and three product codes — 5079, 5080, and 5081.

Within moments, Joule creates the new project opportunity, generates the required quotes for the contractors bidding on the site contract, and organizes all related data within the project opportunity.

Later that morning, when a damaged product report comes in from a client, Adrian simply instructs Joule to raise a credit request.

“A service case has been created and linked to the project,” Joule confirms.

Without opening multiple applications or chasing emails, the issue is automatically routed to customer service with sales order data for processing the credit extracted via AI, ensuring a fast response and complete traceability.

By leveraging SAP Joule, Adrian has transformed what used to be a time-consuming, manual process into an intelligent, automated workflow.

Now, he spends less time managing tasks — and more time building relationships, exploring new opportunities, and driving strategic growth.

🧩 SAP Products That Power Joule Skills

SAP Joule Skills are designed to work seamlessly across multiple SAP solutions, forming a connected ecosystem for business excellence. Here are some key SAP products that enhance and integrate with Joule:

⚙️SAP Build

  • The foundation for creating Joule Skills.
  • Allows both business users and developers to design, automate, and test workflows through a low-code environment.

🧾 SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Central to managing projects, quotes, and sales orders.
  • Joule connects directly with S/4HANA data to fetch customer, product, and pricing details in real time.

💬SAP Sales Cloud

  • Integrates with Joule to streamline the quote-to-order process.
  • Ensures sales teams can generate accurate quotes and track opportunities efficiently.

🤝SAP Customer Experience (CX)

  • Improves collaboration between sales and customer support.
  • Joule automates ticket creation, credit requests, and case tracking for customer satisfaction.

🧰 SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

  • The underlying infrastructure that connects all SAP solutions.
  • Ensures secure, scalable, and high-performance integration between Joule Skills and enterprise systems.

💼Use Case: Project Opportunity & Quote Creation

Let’s look at a real-world example featuring Adrian Chase, a sales professional who uses SAP Joule to manage project opportunities, quotes, and service requests seamlessly.

Step 1: Create a New Project

Arian launches the Joule app from his workspace and starts a new project. Joule asks for key information such as the site name, customer details, and materials to be quoted.

Step 2:Create Opportunity

 

Opportunity created in Sales Cloud V2.

The Same Opportunity  is replicated to S4HANA public cloud as an Project 

 

S4HANA Customer Project replication from V2 Sales Opportunity 

 

Step 3: Create a Quote and Associate It with the Existing Opportunity

 

Every quote created is associated with an existing opportunity

Step 4: Handle Service Requests

Arian needs to issue a credit for a damaged product.

 

Case created is associated with an existing opportunity

 

Final 

 

Video

 

 

🚀Why Joule Skills Matter for Businesses

1. Time Efficiency

  • Automate manual processes like project setup, quote generation, and service requests to save time.

2. Accuracy & Data Consistency

  • Reduce errors through system-driven data capture and validation.

3. Improved Collaboration

  • Connect sales, service, and operations teams through unified data flows.

4. Scalable Innovation

  • Quickly adapt Joule Skills to handle new business needs without complex development.

5. AI-Powered Productivity

  • Leverage SAP Joule’s conversational AI to guide employees through complex workflows.

 

🌈Conclusion

SAP Build Joule Skills empower organizations to automate end-to-end workflows, from project creation to customer service handling. When combined with SAP Build, SAP S/4HANA, Sales Cloud, and CX, Joule becomes a central intelligence hub that drives efficiency, consistency, and growth.

By integrating your business processes into Joule Skills, you can:

  • Reduce operational overhead,
  • Enhance customer satisfaction, and
  • Enable teams to focus on strategic goals.

💡Start building your Joule Skills today — and transform the way your business works with the power of SAP!

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud -Year 2025 in Review
entry.content:
Reflecting on 2025: celebrating AI-first innovations and customer success

Customer service has always been a balancing act between delivering great experiences and managing the cost to serve. Today, AI is transforming this balance by automating routine tasks, empowering service teams with deeper insights and guided flows and helping organizations elevate customer satisfaction. It’s an exciting moment for the industry, and for SAP Service Cloud.

Looking back, 2025 stands out as a year marked by significant customer go-lives and steady adoption of AI enhanced capabilities. Through their work, our customers are reshaping the future of customer service, and we are privileged to support them on this journey. Döhler is a strong example of a customer leveraging SAP CX and multiple AI capabilities across the organization to drive both customer value and operational efficiency. Also Eurogast is another widely adopting SAP CX to improve customer service and deliver what they call “handshake quality”. 

Advancing SAP Service Cloud with an AI-first strategy

Our AI-first strategy continued to be a key pillar for SAP Service Cloud development, with a focus on embedding AI capabilities across the service journey. The latest AI capabilities automate service operations, simplify case management and provide service reps with actionable insights.


Recent innovations such as business information extraction and duplicate detection streamline agents’ work during case handling by reducing manual data entry, preventing duplicate records, and minimizing errors. The automatic linking of business documents to cases enables automatic initiation of actions that can be performed.   

 

SAP Service Cloud AI Duplicate detection

Account synopsis and visual insights like sentiment trends and the sentiment graph give agents an immediate understanding of a customer's situation and emotional tone, enabling them to respond more quickly and provide more informed, empathetic service.

Once a case is closed, the resolution summary offers service teams a structured view of the resolution steps, key metrics, and customer satisfaction. This helps teams learn from completed cases and continuously improve service quality.

New AI innovations are being developed at an accelerated pace, and new features often emerge even before the ink is dry—so stay tuned for more updates on what’s coming next.


AI agents that scale service operations

The future of customer service will be shaped by AI agents and human agents working seamlessly together. In 2025, we continued to advance this vision by expanding our portfolio of AI agents that accelerate issue resolution and reduce the overall cost to serve, while enhancing the experience for both customers and service teams.

The Case Categorization Agent automatically analyzes incoming tickets and assigns them to the right teams based on learned patterns and best practices. This ensures consistent case handling, reduces the manual effort spent on triage, and allows service experts to focus on the issues where they have the deepest expertise and can deliver the most value.
See how Bosch is accelerating ticket management with SAP Service Cloud and Case Categorization agent.

The Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent supports utilities providers by giving customers easy access to common service features and product offerings through a human-like, conversational experience. By handling high-volume inquiries across multiple channels, it significantly reduces operational costs while maintaining the level of service quality customers expect from human agents. The agent is delivered as an API-only offering, allowing utilities companies to integrate it into a wide range of frontend applications.

The Q&A Agent autonomously processes and answers customer questions using existing enterprise data. It interprets natural-language questions, identifies intent, and responds with accurate information drawn from predefined data sources such as knowledge bases, historical tickets, or case records. Administrators can configure specific question types and ensure the agent delivers reliable, contextual answers that reduce the workload on human agents.

Together, these AI agents help service organizations scale more effectively, maintain service levels during peak periods, and free human agents to focus on complex or sensitive interactions that require deeper expertise and empathy. See a video of the AI agents in action.

More AI agent innovations are already in development, and you can expect additional industry-specific and cross-process agents to be introduced throughout the year.

AI innovations in SAP Service Cloud


Accelerating issue to resolution process with enhanced Case Management

We continue to receive excellent customer feedback on our guided resolution process, which helps service teams navigate cases more efficiently and consistently. In 2025, we expanded the capabilities with several new features that simplify ticket handling and further accelerate resolution.

Improve case categorization with business information extraction

Building on our AI capabilities, case categorization is now more accurate and automated. Business information extraction helps identify key details within case content, improving categorization quality and reducing the manual effort required during initial triage.


Automate follow-up processes with action steps

A new action step allows service teams to trigger the next step in a process—whether it occurs inside SAP Service Cloud or in an external system. Internally, action steps can automate the creation of follow-up items such as subcases or service orders. Externally, action steps can invoke an external hook to call another system directly from the case flow. This brings greater automation and ensures follow-up processes happen consistently and on time.

Focus on what matters most with personalized KPI cards
Service teams can now tailor KPI cards on the home page to surface the metrics most relevant to their daily work. With visualizations such as bar charts, donut charts, and other formats, agents gain clearer insight into priorities, backlog trends, and workload distribution.

SAP Service Cloud KPI cards


Empowering agents with a more connected and insightful workspace

For service teams, it is essential that agents have all relevant information at their fingertips and can efficiently handle customer inquiries across any channel. In 2025, we introduced several enhancements to the Agent Desktop that strengthen omnichannel support and streamline the way agents respond to customer needs.

Amazon Connect chatbot integration
To strengthen self-service capabilities, SAP Service Cloud now integrates with Amazon Connect chatbots. Customers can resolve common inquiries through conversational self-service before ever reaching an agent, reducing wait times and lowering operational effort. When a handover to a human agent is needed, the Agent Desktop provides full chatbot context, allowing agents to pick up the conversation seamlessly without requiring customers to repeat information. This creates a smoother, more efficient experience for both customers and service teams.

Letter interactions
Despite increasing digitalization, letters remain an important communication channel in many industries. Agents can now manage letter-based communications directly within the Agent Desktop, ensuring formal correspondence is handled as seamlessly as digital interactions. With the integration of OpenText Document Presentment Service, agents can quickly create outbound letters using automatically prepopulated fields and brand-aligned templates.

Keep agents informed
Customer Hub provides service agents with the information they need to deliver personalized and efficient experiences. We have added several enhancements to further improve usability and quick access to relevant information. Internal alerts are one example: they notify service reps about payment issues, escalations, or other important customer updates.


End to end issue resolution made easier  

We have enhanced our integration with SAP S/4HANA in many ways, improving visibility and information flow across systems. Wit the recent customer returns integration you can capture the customer return orders and replicate to SAP S/4HANA for further processing, ensuring transparency throughout the process. We have further improved the Service Order integration for a more streamlined inhouse repair process.

The direct integration with SAP Field Service Management streamlines issue resolution from the first contact all the way to scheduling and the onsite fix, helping teams coordinate more effectively and increase first time fix.

We have also enabled URL mashups in case management and service order, making it easier to connect SAP Service Cloud with any systems and processes needed for successful service delivery.

 

Strengthening service capabilities for utilities providers

This year, we introduced a new GenAI-powered Utilities Insights scenario that helps service agents quickly understand key consumption trends for a premise. With AI-generated summaries of consumption graphs, agents can immediately identify usage fluctuations, anomalies, and important patterns—supporting more informed conversations and faster issue resolution for utilities customers.

Beyond AI insights, we also delivered several enhancements that give utilities agents deeper visibility into operational and account-related conditions. Agents can now easily see when a premise is disconnected and, where applicable, initiate a reconnection of AMI meters directly from SAP Service Cloud through seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA. We also added clearer visibility into contract account blocks and contract blocks and locks, enabling agents to understand any restrictions on billing or service processes without switching views.

To simplify navigation and accelerate access to the right information, Utilities Entities are now presented in a hierarchical structure—collective contract account, contract account, premise, and contract—allowing agents to understand customer context at a glance.


Shaping customer service for next year and the years ahead

As we look ahead, the future of customer service will be shaped by human agents and AI agents working seamlessly together. AI will increasingly handle routine tasks, surface insights, and guide service flows, while human experts focus on the complex, empathetic interactions that truly build customer trust. We are excited to continue advancing SAP Service Cloud in this direction, working closely with our customers and partners to deliver innovations that enhance service operations, improve customer experiences, and empower teams to do their best work. Together, we are shaping a new era of intelligent, connected customer service.

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud release highlights Q4/2025
entry.content:
What is new in SAP Service Cloud? 


Explore a selection of our new features in this blog post and join our webcasts for live demos and deeper insights SAP Service Cloud Version 2 updates. 

Register for our Q4 2025 customer webcast: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5190303/34D941B16A9727E33187B9FA4DB3DEF7  

Read the SAP CX release highlights blog here

As we begin 2026, let’s take a look at the capabilities delivered over the past months and how they improve service efficiency and drive customer satisfaction.


AI agent innovations: Faster self-service with intelligent assistance

Let’s start by taking a look at the new AI agents available for customer service.  As customer service continues to evolve toward closer collaboration between AI and human agents, we are expanding our AI agent portfolio. Our latest AI agent innovations help customers resolve issues faster through self-service while reducing manual effort for service teams.

Digital Service Agent

The Digital Service Agent provides AI-driven, conversational support that helps customers resolve questions quickly through self-service channels using relevant, knowledge-based responses. It reduces customer effort by delivering instant, informed assistance and seamlessly hands over to human agents when needed. By handling routine and high-volume inquiries, the agent helps lower support costs and allows service teams to focus on more complex customer needs.

 


The Utilities Customer Self Service Agent

The Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent supports utilities providers by giving customers easy access to common service features and product offerings through a human-like, conversational experience. By handling high-volume inquiries across multiple channels, it reduces operational costs while maintaining the level of service quality customers expect from human agents. The agent is delivered as an API-only offering, allowing utilities companies to integrate it into a wide range of frontend applications.

 



SAP Service Cloud innovations in Q4: Productivity, visibility, and smarter service workflows

 

Simplify agent workflows with WalkMe in-app guidance

WalkMe, an AI-powered digital adoption platform, is now integrated into SAP Service Cloud to provide intuitive, in-app guidance for service agents. With WalkMe, teams can create step-by-step walkthroughs and contextual tips directly within the application. This simplifies the agent experience, accelerates onboarding, and provides visibility into where agents encounter friction—helping organizations continuously optimize their service processes.


Case Management: Create visits directly from a case

Based on customer feedback, we have added the ability to create a follow-up visit directly from a case. This enhancement eliminates manual steps and improves workflow efficiency, helping teams schedule and manage onsite visits more easily and ensure seamless handoffs throughout the service process.


Case Management: Customize KPI cards on Homepage for more efficient case handling

Service teams can now tailor KPI cards on the home page to surface the metrics most relevant to their daily work. With visualizations such as bar charts, donut charts, and other formats, agents gain clearer visibility into priorities and workload distribution, helping them quickly identify where attention is needed.


Customer timeline: Add external business events directly in the timeline

You can now add and display business events from external systems directly in the customer timeline. These events appear alongside emails, interactions, and notes in the standard application, giving service teams a more complete view of relevant activities without switching between applications.

Recent items: Quickly access frequently used objects

Based on customer feedback, we have introduced the Recent items feature to help service agents save time in their daily work. Agents can now access their most recently used objects directly from the application menu, from any page within SAP Service Cloud. This makes it easy to return to ongoing tasks or recently viewed records—even after a page refresh—reducing navigation effort and improving efficiency during day-to-day service activities.

 
Utilities: Gain rapid insights with Generative AI summaries for consumption data

We introduced a new gen AI scenario for Utilities Insights that enables service agents to quickly understand key consumption trends for a premise. With AI-generated summaries of consumption graphs, agents can immediately identify usage fluctuations, anomalies, and important patterns—supporting more informed conversations and faster resolution for utilities customers.
New Generative AI Scenario: Utilities Insights | SAP Help Portal

In addition to AI insights, several new features now give utilities agents improved visibility into operational and account-related conditions. Agents can easily see when a premise is disconnected and, where applicable, initiate a reconnection of AMI meters directly from SAP Service Cloud, leveraging seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA.

We have also added clear visibility into contract account locks and contract locks and blocks, helping agents understand any restrictions on billing or service processes right from the contract account view. These enhancements help agents stay better informed and support faster, more confident decision-making during customer interactions.


More information

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources: 

  • SAP Service Cloud V2 What’s New  
  • SAP Service Cloud V2 Release Navigator 
  • Release webcast: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5190303/34D941B16A9727E33187B9FA4DB3DEF7  

 

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Sales and Service Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Enhancing Customer Data Accuracy with SAP DQM Address Validation in CX Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Why Address Quality Matters in Customer Experience

In today’s customer-centric world, accurate address data is far more than a backend requirement — it directly impacts customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and compliance. Invalid or inconsistent addresses lead to failed deliveries, delayed services, poor agent productivity, and frustrated customers.

With SAP CX Service Cloud V2, organizations can significantly improve data quality by integrating SAP Data Quality Management (DQM) – Address Validation Service, ensuring that customer addresses are accurate, standardized, and validated in real time.

What is SAP DQM – Address Validation?

SAP DQM Address Validation is a cloud-based service that helps organizations:

  • Validate postal addresses in real time
  • Standardize address formats based on country-specific rules
  • Enrich address data with missing components
  • Reduce duplicates and incorrect records

The service supports global address validation across multiple countries, making it ideal for enterprises operating at scale.

Why Integrate DQM with CX Service Cloud V2?

CX Service Cloud V2 acts as the single source of truth for customer interactions. Ensuring clean address data at this layer brings several benefits:

Key Business Benefits

  • 🚀Faster case handling with fewer address-related corrections
  • 📦Reduced delivery failures and service disruptions
  • 😊Improved customer trust and experience
  • 📊Better reporting, analytics, and segmentation

Key Technical Benefits

  • Real-time validation during data entry
  • API-based integration aligned with SAP BTP standards
  • Reusable validation logic across multiple CX touchpoints

Prerequisites

Before enabling the DQM service, ensure the following are in place:

  • SAP BTP account (Cloud Foundry environment)
  • Entitlement to SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data
  • CX Service Cloud V2 tenant
  • Basic understanding of SAP BTP Destinations and API consumption
  • SAP Build Apps

Enable DQM Service in SAP BTP

  • Navigate to SAP BTP Cockpit
  • Go to Entitlements → Entity Assignments
  • Add entitlement for SAP Data Quality Management
  • Create a Service Instance for Address Validation

Configure Authentication & Access

DQM services are secured using OAuth 2.0:

  • Create a Service Key for the DQM instance
  • Capture:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
    • Token URL
    • API endpoint

These credentials are used by CX extensions or middleware to securely call the DQM API.

Create Destination in SAP BTP

Create an HTTP destination to consume the DQM service:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • Token Service URL: From service key
  • Client ID / Secret: From service key
  • Target URL: DQM Address Validation endpoint

This destination enables secure and reusable connectivity from:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • Custom CX extensions

Similarly create for SAP Service Cloud V2

Create SAP Build Apps Project

  • Open SAP Build Apps
  • Create a new project (Web or Responsive App)
  • Choose Blank App or Master-Detail template
  • Name it: CX_Address_Validation_App
  • Design your site as per you requirement 

Create Mashup in CX Service Cloud V2

  • Log in to CX Service Cloud V2
  • Navigate to:
    Administrator → Mashup Authoring
  • Click New Mashup
  • Provide:
    • Name: Address Validation – DQM
    • Type: URL Mashup
    • URL: Build Apps deployed URL

✔ Save the mashup.

Embedded Mashup in Account - CX Service Cloud V2

For a detailed walkthrough of the functionality, please refer to the video.
It showcases the embedded address validation experience in CX Service Cloud V2.

entry.title: Release Navigator for Customer Experience – Q1 2026 Update
entry.content:
Hello CX Community! 

As we begin another year of innovation across the Customer Experience (CX) portfolio, we are excited to share that the release navigator for Customer Experience is now complete for Q1 2026.  

With every new release, a wide range of information, documentation, and resources are updated and made available for you to review. Our goal is to provide timely enablement to help users get ready for the latest release cycle. 

 

Tool Overview

If you are not yet familiar with the release navigator for Customer Experience, it is a resource provided by SAP Enterprise Support designed to offer the following: 

  • A central repository for current product information 
  • A unified access point for all customer experience release materials, including documentation, blogs, webinars, schedules, e-learnings and newsletters 
  • Streamlined access to release details across SAP solutions through additional release navigators 

Content Insights 

Q1 brings updated release information across SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, SAP Engagement Cloud previously known as SAP Emarsys, and SAP Marketing Cloud.  Products following a continuous delivery model: SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Commerce Cloud also feature updated Q4 recap content.  

Several important updates have been introduced in this quarter’s release navigator.  

New Release Blog Posts for SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 

New release blog posts are available for SAP Sales and SAP Service Cloud Version 2. These blogs provide a concise summary of the most significant enhancements delivered each quarter. They are designed to give teams a quick and comprehensive overview of the latest updates. 

Enhancements to the General Page 

The General page within the release navigator has been expanded to include new content. Notably, the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and the CX webcast page have been added, offering users enhanced access to educational resources and support materials directly through the navigator. 

Changes to SAP Marketing Cloud  

Release webcasts for SAP Marketing Cloud will be discontinued as this product is in the process of being retired. Despite this change, SAP remains committed to supporting customers during the transition period by continuing to provide minor enhancement updates. 

New content on SAP Commerce Cloud

We enhance the already packed SAP Commerce Cloud product page with additional items like: the Storefront Maturity Assesment , which offers actionable recommendations and evaluates readiness to shift from Accelerator to a headless storefront, a video with insights on future commerce transformation with Agentic AI.

The SAP Engagement Cloud (SAP Emarsys) Q1 2026 release content will be available as usual on the release day, February 19.

Innovation Schedule 

Following the established rhythm from previous years, the release navigator will continue to release content updates quarterly ahead of the planned CX product releases in February, May, August, and November. This ensures you always have the latest information at your fingertips to keep projects running smoothly.  

In addition, new user- experience updates will follow a single annual update cycle, delivered in August along with the Q3 content update. This approach supports a stable and predictable evolution of the platform’s look and feel.  

Informational Timeline: 

 Disclaimer: These dates are for informational purposes only and may change. The Readiness@Scale team has the final decision over any UX modifications.

Connect with Us

Your feedback is essential to shaping future improvements to the release navigator. We invite you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or requests in the comments section
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional content

Thank you for your continued engagement and support.

Best Run,
Readiness@Scale

SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: SAP Customer Experience Value Packages
entry.content:
A sales leader needs better pipeline visibility.
An e-commerce manager sees customers abandon their carts.
A service team struggles to meet response targets.

These situations are common and they often reveal where the customer experience starts to break down.

The SAP Customer Experience Value Map helps you address these challenges through focused Value Packages. Each one is built around a real business goal and shows how SAP solutions support better outcomes through practical capabilities and guided learning.

To get started, explore the blog available in the Value Map for each scenario. It introduces the challenge, highlights key opportunities, and sets the context for improvement.

👉 Explore the SAP Customer Experience Value Packages and begin your learning journey

 
Each Value Package is designed to help you connect business goals with the capabilities available in SAP Customer Experience solutions. By exploring the blogs and the learning content in our new Learning Platform, you can better understand how these capabilities support improvements in sales efficiency, digital commerce, and customer service.

We encourage you to start with the blog that introduces the business challenge and the opportunity behind each topic. From there, you can continue with the Value Package learning content to explore the capabilities in more detail and see how they can support your organization’s CX priorities.

 💡  Have questions or feedback on this content?

Explore the SAP Customer Experience Value Map, which combines expert-led guidance with self-paced learning materials:

  • Blogs and Articles – Stay up to date with the latest enablement stories, tips, and insights from SAP experts.
  • Event Calendar – Join live sessions, webinars, and workshops designed to help you learn directly from specialists.
  • Best Practices and Guides – Access ready-to-use materials that simplify complex CX topics.
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC) Services – Keep your SAP CX systems running efficiently.

entry.title: Shape the Future of AI: Join the SAP Enterprise Service Management – Hands-on Experience
entry.content:
Are you ready to influence the next generation of SAP-managed integrations?

We are excited to announce two unique opportunities for customers and partners to get hands-on experience with
SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
We are looking for participants to collaborate with us, validate new features, and provide early feedback that will directly impact
SAP’s product roadmap.
Whether you are focused on the technical "how-to" of onboarding or the functional power of Generative AI, we have a track for you. 

Choose Your Path
We are offering two SAP Integration Experience Feedback (IEF) tracks for our customers and partners.
You can participate in one or both, depending on their expertise:

Track 1: Provisioning & Setup (The Implementation Deep-Dive)

Focus on the end-to-end journey of getting the system up and running.

  • What you’ll do: Experience the SAP-managed integration and E2E customer onboarding, including SAP for Me
    and SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC).
  • The Goal: Help us automate and reduce manual effort in the Cloud ALM experience.
  • Best for: Project Leads, Business Process Consultants, and Technical Experts.
  • For Track 1 please register here.

Track 2: Functional & embedded AI Experience (The Business User Deep-Dive)

Focus on the business process and the power of embedded AI.

  • What you’ll do: Validate the "Backend Entity Extraction" feature - a Generative AI tool that automatically identifies
    dispute amounts, currencies, and reasons from emails.
  • The Goal: Evaluate AI Quality (Reliability, Relevance, and Robustness) and test the seamless data flow between
    SAP Enterprise Service Management and SAP S/4HANA     Cloud Public Edition.
  • Best for: Business Process Experts and those familiar with Dispute Management and Master Data Synchronization.
  • For Track 2 please register here.

Why Participate?

  • First-Hand Access: Get early access to the AI-embedded Dispute Management scenario
    as part of SAP Enterprise Service Management.
  • Influence SAP: Your feedback directly improves the quality and direction of SAP-managed integrations.
  • Recognition: Receive a confirmation of participation from SAP upon completion.

Engagement Details

  • Timeline: April – October 2026
  • Commitment: Includes an initial briefing, a kick-off demo, and 2 - 3 days of hands-on activities in a provided
    SAP landscape.
  • Location: Worldwide (Remote sessions)
  • Prerequisite: A valid Feedback Agreement (FBA) with SAP is required (if not available, we can help you set this up after registration!).


Looking foward to receive your registrations!
Your SAP Integration Experience Feedback Team

entry.title: Reimagining the Customer Journey with AI-Powered SAP Sales and Service Excellence
entry.content:
📌  Introduction

How modern enterprises are weaving together intelligent automation, real-time knowledge, and seamless cross-platform workflows to deliver frictionless customer experiences.

In today’s digital-first world, customers expect fast, personalized, and seamless service experiences. Traditional disconnected systems often lead to delays, inefficiencies, and poor customer satisfaction.

To address this, organizations are adopting intelligent, integrated solutions that connect sales, service, and backend operations. By leveraging platforms like SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP Field Service Management, along with AI capabilities, businesses can deliver a unified and proactive customer journey.

This blog demonstrates an end-to-end process that integrates AI, automation, and enterprise applications to optimize customer experience from initiation to closure.

💼  Business Use Case

Consider a scenario where a customer encounters an issue with a product and begins by attempting to resolve it through a self-service portal. If the issue remains unresolved, a case is created and handled by a service agent, supported by intelligent recommendations and knowledge systems. As the interaction progresses, the situation may evolve into a potential sales opportunity, enabling the organization to propose a suitable solution or upgrade. A quote is then generated and shared with the customer, who can review and sign it digitally, ensuring a seamless and efficient agreement process.

Once confirmed, backend systems take over to process the order and initiate service fulfillment. This includes creating the necessary orders and coordinating with field service operations, where a technician is assigned for on-site support. The technician completes the required service, resolves the issue, and updates the system, leading to the closure of the case.

This end-to-end use case demonstrates how organizations can transform traditional support interactions into revenue-generating opportunities, while automating backend processes, improving response and resolution times, and delivering a truly seamless, cross-functional customer experience.

🏗️   Architecture

The solution integrates multiple systems across the customer journey:


Frontend & Experience Layer
SAP Build Apps
• Self-service capabilities for customers
Customer Engagement Layer
SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2
• Case Management • Opportunity Management • Quote Management • AI-assisted troubleshooting
AI & Knowledge Layer
eGain Knowledge Management
• Intelligent Q&A • Knowledge Base search • Similar case suggestions
Digital Signature Integration
DocuSign
• Seamless agreement processing • Digital contract signing
Backend ERP Layer
SAP S/4HANA Cloud
• Solution Order • Sales Order • Delivery • Billing
Field Service Layer
SAP Field Service Management
• Service Call • Technician Assignment • Field execution

⚙️  Key Capabilities and Business Benefits

  • 🤖AI-Driven Self-Service : Automated issue identification and resolution suggestions
  • 📚Intelligent Knowledge Management : Context-aware recommendations using knowledge base
  • 🔄End-to-End Process Integration : Seamless flow from case → opportunity → quote → order → service
  • ✍️Digital Contract Management : Faster approvals using DocuSign
  • 🔧Smart Field Service Execution : Automated technician assignment and scheduling

📋  Prerequisites

Before implementing this end-to-end solution, it’s important to have the right landscape, integrations, and development setup in place.

🔧  Core Systems

Ensure access to the following key solutions:

  • SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 for case, opportunity, and quote management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for backend order processing and billing
  • SAP Field Service Management for service execution and technician handling

🔌  Integration Layer

  • Middleware or API-based integration using SAP BTP (recommended)
  • Connectivity with DocuSign for digital agreements
  • Integration with eGain Knowledge Management for knowledge and AI-driven recommendations

🧠 AI Enablement

  • Configured and structured knowledge base
  • AI services enabled for intelligent Q&A, recommendations, and case insights

👨‍💻Development & Extension Setup

  • Use CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) to build custom microservices, autoflow logic, and backend extensions on SAP BTP
  • Develop UI applications using SAP Fiori for a consistent and enterprise-ready user experience
  • Expose and consume APIs for seamless communication between frontend, AI services, and backend systems

The step journey, unpacked

The flow is built around four macro-phases — Initiate, Assess & Troubleshoot, Resolve, and Closure — each supported by a distinct set of SAP and partner tools.

Step 1: Self-service

The customer lands on a self-service portal before ever reaching a human. Intelligent Q&A, powered by eGain Knowledge Management, deflects simple queries instantly.

Step 2 : Case creation

When self-service can't resolve the issue, a case is created in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2. AI surfaces similar past cases and searches the knowledge base automatically.

Step 3: Opportunity

A service case becomes a commercial opportunity. Agents can pivot from problem-solving mode to solution-selling without switching systems.

Step 4 : Quote + DocuSign

A quote is generated and sent for e-signature via DocuSign — all within the same workflow. No email attachments, no context switching.

 

Step 5 : Solution order

In our Scenario we are using Sales order and Service Order

Once signed, the solution order in S/4HANA  is created and downstream fulfilment is triggered automatically.

 

Step 6 : Sales order fulfilment

SAP S/4HANA Cloud handles the sales order, delivery, and billing — a direct integration that eliminates re-keying of data.

Step 7 : Service order

In parallel, a service order is raised for any physical work that needs to happen on-site.

Step 8 : Service order replication

The service order is replicated into SAP Field Service Management, keeping every system consistent without manual effort.

Step 9 : Service call

A service call is initiated in SAP FSM, formally opening the field engagement.

 

Step 10 : Technician assignment

The right technician is assigned based on availability, skills, and location — the field team arrives prepared and resolve this issue

🏁Conclusion

This end-to-end flow demonstrates how organizations can transform customer journeys using AI and integrated enterprise platforms. By connecting sales, service, and backend operations, businesses can deliver faster, smarter, and more efficient customer experiences.

entry.title: DebugSheet: Get XML Data for Local Print Form Preview in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer
entry.content:
Overview of my Print Form Resources

I decided to create a separate article to avoid duplicate content in each print form blog post and simplify the maintenance. You can find the list of print form-related Blogs, information on Live Sessions and an Onboarding Video for beginners in the following blog post:

Print Form Adaptation with Adobe LiveCycle Designer Overview - Onboarding, Blogs and Live Session

 

Intro

When working on print forms in Adobe LiveCycle Designer, having XML data (sometimes called sample data) to preview the form locally is invaluable. Here are some of the key benefits:

  • No need to upload to preview: You can check your current layout and verify that adjustments render correctly — all without uploading the form and opening the target business object in the system again and again after each little adjustment.
  • Field identification: With real sample data from your system, you can compare the output directly to the system screen and locate fields by their content, which is especially helpful when you don’t know a field’s exact technical name.
  • Local data manipulation: You can modify data locally — extend a text, insert line breaks, duplicate items, and so on — to see how the form behaves with different data sets.

Bottom line: Nothing beats real sample data from the target system. 💪

 

How to get XML Data in SAP systems?

The ways to obtain that XML data vary significantly across SAP solutions and often even differ by business object within the same solution. This guide applies to SAP solutions where forms are rendered via the Adobe Document Services (ADS) and customized using Adobe LiveCycle Designer. These include:

  • SAP Sales/Service Cloud (Version 1, formerly SAP Cloud for Customer)
  • SAP Sales/Service Cloud Version 2
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Business ByDesign

The approach described here is often a last resort, as it involves some effort — if a system offers better ways, use those 😉
However, the DebugSheet provides an unfiltered look behind the scenes.

Important: Since this method requires replacing the currently active form template in the system, it should never be used in production environments!

 

Procedure

  1. Copy the content of the XML code block from the end of this blog into a text editor and save the file as DebugSheet2.xdp
    This workaround is necessary because uploading .xdp files is not supported here.
  2. Next, upload the file as the template for the desired document in your system. Here’s where to do that in each solution:
    • SAP Sales/Service Cloud: Work Center Administrator → View Form Template Maintenance
    • SAP Sales/Service Cloud Version 2: System Settings → Form Templates → (All Settings) → (Output Management) → Maintain Form Template
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Output Management Form Template app
    • SAP Business ByDesign: Work Center Application and User Management → View Form Template Maintenance

  3. Don’t forget to publish/activate the template and, if applicable, configure any rules that ensure the template is actually used for output.
  4. Now navigate to the relevant business object or app in the system and trigger the PDF generation.
    The DebugSheet will appear, displaying the XML data.

 

A word of caution when copying: Copying text from PDF files is generally not as seamless as copying from, say, a Word document. For example, selecting and copying text across multiple pages in a PDF often pulls in unwanted content from headers, footers, or other overlapping elements. The DebugSheet uses a layout that is optimized for copying XML content spanning multiple pages, but you may still encounter issues with truncated or wrapped lines. At the very least, however, you can search for specific fields and their values to verify whether certain values are part of the data set at all and could therefore be displayed in the PDF.

 

Behind the Scenes

Extracting the XML data is no rocket science. The DebugSheet essentially leverages a built-in function and outputs the result in an optimized layout. The following line of JavaScript does the heavy lifting:

this.rawValue = xfa.data.saveXML("pretty");

Here, this should reference a text field that allows line breaks and whose height expands automatically to fit the content.

 

DebugSheet2.xdp

Copy the code block's content to a text editor and save it as DebugSheet2.xdp. This workaround is necessary because uploading .xdp files is not supported here. (Do not use Word or similar programs, since they don't save plain text files.)

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<?xfa generator="AdobeLiveCycleDesigner_V11.0.9.20230825.1.45_SAP" APIVersion="3.6.23237.0"?>

<template xmlns:xliff="urn:oasis:names:tc:xliff:document:1.1" xmlns="http://www.xfa.org/schema/xfa-template/2.8/">

   <?formServer allowRenderCaching 0?>
   <?formServer formModel both?>

                     </exData>

         <?templateDesigner expand 0?></pageSet>
      <subform w="8in" name="bdy_Page" layout="tb">
         <subform w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" layout="tb" name="frmInfo">

                  </fill>

               </ui>
               <font typeface="Arial"/>
               <margin topInset="1mm" bottomInset="1mm" leftInset="1mm" rightInset="1mm"/>
               <para vAlign="middle"/>
               <caption reserve="25mm">
                  <para vAlign="middle"/>
                  <value>
                     <text xliff:rid="CDB41F6E-8154-4C44-8ACF-95C28ABC6C04">Date/Time Field</text>
                  </value>
               </caption>
               <format>
                  <picture>datetime.full{}</picture>
               </format>
               <event activity="initialize" name="event__initialize">
                  <script contentType="application/x-javascript">
this.rawValue = xfa.host.currentDateTime();

</script>
               </event>
            </field>
            <field name="txtHostInformation" w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" access="readOnly">
               <ui>
                  <textEdit multiLine="1">
                     <border>
                        <?templateDesigner StyleID aped3?>
                        <edge stroke="lowered"/>
                     </border>
                     <margin/>
                  </textEdit>
               </ui>
               <font typeface="Arial"/>
               <margin topInset="1mm" bottomInset="1mm" leftInset="1mm" rightInset="1mm"/>
               <para vAlign="middle"/>
               <bind match="none"/>
               <event activity="initialize" name="event__initialize">
                  <script contentType="application/x-javascript">
this.rawValue = "DateTime:\t" + dtNow.rawValue + "\n" +
"   Formatted:\t" + dtNow.formattedValue + "\n" +
"   Pattern:\t" + dtNow.format.picture.value + "\n" +
"   Locale:\t" + dtNow.locale + "\n" +
"Name:\t\t" + xfa.host.name + "\n" +
"Version:\t" + xfa.host.version + "\n" +
"Platform:\t" + xfa.host.platform + "\n" +
"Host Lang.:\t" + xfa.host.language;

this.rawValue += "\nHost Title:\t" + xfa.host.title;
</script>
               </event>
            </field>
            <bind match="none"/>
            <?templateDesigner expand 1?></subform>
         <subform w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" layout="tb" name="frmDebug">
            <field name="txtDebug" w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" presence="hidden">
               <ui>
                  <textEdit>
                     <border>
                        <?templateDesigner StyleID aped3?>
                        <edge stroke="lowered"/>
                     </border>
                     <margin/>
                  </textEdit>
               </ui>
               <font typeface="Arial"/>
               <margin topInset="1mm" bottomInset="1mm" leftInset="1mm" rightInset="1mm"/>
               <para vAlign="middle"/>
               <value>
                  <text xliff:rid="AB0D916A-DAD3-4139-A4F9-DD2C4FA08241">DEBUG</text>
               </value>
               <border>
                  <edge presence="hidden"/>
                  <fill>
                     <color value="255,204,153"/>
                  </fill>
               </border>
               <bind match="none"/>
            </field>
            <?templateDesigner expand 1?></subform>
         <subform y="16mm" w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" layout="tb" name="frmDataSource">
            <draw name="lblDataSource" w="203.2mm" h="7mm">
               <ui>
                  <textEdit/>
               </ui>
               <value>
                  <text xliff:rid="8D7B9F04-8587-4E36-904E-576E51709E41">DataSource</text>
               </value>
               <font typeface="Arial"/>
               <margin topInset="0.5mm" bottomInset="0.5mm" leftInset="0.5mm" rightInset="0.5mm"/>
               <para vAlign="middle" hAlign="center"/>
               <border>
                  <edge>
                     <color value="192,192,192"/>
                  </edge>
                  <corner thickness="0.176mm">
                     <color value="192,192,192"/>
                  </corner>
                  <fill>
                     <color value="204,255,204"/>
                  </fill>
               </border>
               <?renderCache.bounds 573166 17009 0 0 1417 1417 0 0?>
               <?renderCache.textRun 10 DataSource 0 261596 12817 0 0 0 "Arial" 0 0 10000 ISO-8859-1?></draw>
            <field name="txtContent" w="203.2mm" minH="9mm" access="readOnly">
               <ui>
                  <textEdit multiLine="1">
                     <border>
                        <?templateDesigner StyleID aped3?>
                        <edge stroke="lowered"/>
                     </border>
                     <margin/>
                  </textEdit>
               </ui>

               <margin topInset="1mm" bottomInset="1mm" leftInset="1mm" rightInset="1mm"/>
               <para vAlign="middle"/>

                  <edge presence="hidden"/>
               </border>
               <value>
                  <text> </text>
               </value>
               <bind match="none"/>
               <event activity="initialize" name="event__initialize">
                  <script contentType="application/x-javascript">
this.rawValue = xfa.data.saveXML("pretty");
</script>
               </event>
            </field>
            <bind match="none"/>
            <?templateDesigner expand 1?></subform>
         <bind match="none"/>
         <?templateDesigner expand 1?></subform>
